jokes.text: I just took the most incredible dump. I mean, I haven't had one like that since-- I believe-- Reagan was in the White House.
jokes.setup: Hank describes having the most perfect morning with poetic language about nature
jokes.punchline: The culmination of this perfect morning is an incredible bowel movement, dated to a presidential administration
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - only Hank would overshare this intimate detail with such earnest poetry. The Reagan reference adds specificity and absurd precision to bathroom talk.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I just took the most incredible dump. I mean, I haven't had one like that since-- I believe-- Reagan was in the White House.

jokes.text: Bad things coming in threes. First my divorce, then the pool filter catching on fire, and now this.
jokes.setup: Hank tries to philosophically contextualize his agent's heart attack
jokes.punchline: He equates a pool filter fire with divorce and potential death
jokes.explanation: Reveals Hank's self-centered worldview and poor sense of proportion. The pool filter is perfectly random and mundane.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Bad things coming in threes. First my divorce, then the pool filter catching on fire, and now this.

jokes.text: Hmm? I said good morning. Ok. God! Don't be a prick about it.
jokes.setup: Normal morning greeting exchange
jokes.punchline: Paula immediately escalates to hostile name-calling over a simple greeting
jokes.explanation: Shows Paula's bad mood and workplace tension, but fairly standard irritation comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Hmm? I said good morning. Ok. God! Don't be a prick about it.

jokes.text: Oh, look. Bitch expo '95 is in town. Could you pick me up a tee shirt, ok?
jokes.setup: Phil responding to workplace tension
jokes.punchline: Creates fake convention name to describe the office atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Clever fictional convention name, though the concept is somewhat predictable workplace humor.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Oh, look. Bitch expo '95 is in town. Could you pick me up a tee shirt, ok?

jokes.text: Is your grandmother alive? What? Is she alive? Yes. Well, fuck her. I'll get to Jimmy Smits when I get to it.
jokes.setup: Phil is being pressured about booking Jimmy Smits
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about grandmother followed by bizarre curse
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd non-sequitur that reveals workplace frustration through completely illogical aggression.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Is your grandmother alive? What? Is she alive? Yes. Well, fuck her. I'll get to Jimmy Smits when I get to it.

jokes.text: I have a lump in my breast.
jokes.setup: Beverly trying to understand Paula's bad attitude
jokes.punchline: Serious medical revelation that recontextualizes everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - reveals the real reason behind her behavior. Economy of language creates maximum impact shift.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I have a lump in my breast.

jokes.text: Don't hug, because then it feels serious and cancerous.
jokes.setup: Beverly's natural impulse to comfort Paula
jokes.punchline: Paula's practical reasoning about emotional responses to cancer scares
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about dealing with medical scares. Paula's logic is both practical and emotionally avoidant.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Don't hug, because then it feels serious and cancerous.

jokes.text: And please stop staring at my tits.
jokes.setup: Paula has just revealed her breast lump
jokes.punchline: Calls out Beverly's natural but inappropriate curiosity
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable truth about human nature when someone mentions medical issues involving specific body parts.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: And please stop staring at my tits.

jokes.text: It's not just the fact that I might die, but my doctor said I can't have coffee anymore... It's turning me into a colossal bitch.
jokes.setup: Paula explaining why she's been difficult
jokes.punchline: Coffee withdrawal is presented as equally serious as potential death
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals character priorities and explains her behavior through relatable caffeine addiction.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: It's not just the fact that I might die, but my doctor said I can't have coffee anymore... It's turning me into a colossal bitch.

jokes.text: I'll look through this hole... Is that close enough? Is that your thumb? What is that?
jokes.setup: Larry getting massage, Phil trying to show him monologue jokes
jokes.punchline: Awkward positioning creates unintentionally suggestive situation
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy awkwardness. Functional but not particularly clever or character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I'll look through this hole...

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Is that close enough?

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Is that your thumb?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What is that?

jokes.text: Is that newt gingrich on the internet? No, what she's doing to my back.
jokes.setup: Phil asking about what Larry is looking at
jokes.punchline: Larry's misdirection about political figure vs. massage technique
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection joke with 90s political reference. Structure works but not deeply character-driven.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Is that newt gingrich on the internet?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, what she's doing to my back.

jokes.text: I was just getting ready to turn over, and sometimes the towel flips up. I've already seen that.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to entice Phil to stay
jokes.punchline: Phil's matter-of-fact response that he's seen Larry naked before
jokes.explanation: Workplace familiarity taken to uncomfortable extreme. Phil's casualness about it makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I was just getting ready to turn over, and sometimes the towel flips up.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I've already seen that.

jokes.text: Hey, dad. Asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry greeting someone warmly
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows up with casual insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders character moment - affectionate but immediately undermining. Great economy.

dialogue.line: Hey, dad.
Asshole.

jokes.text: What, were you in vietnam?
jokes.setup: Someone giving Larry an overly aggressive massage
jokes.punchline: Larry's hyperbolic comparison to war trauma
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from massage complaint to military trauma reference. Typical Larry hyperbole.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What, were you in vietnam?

jokes.text: I think Brett Butler's in there.
jokes.setup: Hank worried about Larry's office being left vulnerable
jokes.punchline: The specific threat is a female comedian
jokes.explanation: Hank's paranoia about celebrities invading Larry's space. Brett Butler as specific threat is mildly amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I think Brett Butler's in there.

jokes.text: Oh, damn. The day can turn to shit so quickly.
jokes.setup: Hank's morning started perfectly (with the great dump)
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about how fast things deteriorate
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to opening scene. Hank's tendency toward dramatic overstatement about minor inconveniences.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, damn. The day can turn to shit so quickly.

jokes.text: The Butler did it.
jokes.setup: Asked who put the best sitcom on TV last year
jokes.punchline: Murder mystery reference that's also Brett Butler's name
jokes.explanation: Classic pun structure works well. Not groundbreaking but solid wordplay execution.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The Butler did it.

jokes.text: How do you come up with that stuff? Oh, my gifts are many and best not discussed.
jokes.setup: Brett asking about Artie's comedic abilities
jokes.punchline: Artie's mock-humble boasting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie line - false modesty that's actually bragging. Great character voice and quotable phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: How do you come up with that stuff?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, my gifts are many and best not discussed.

jokes.text: Can I just peek under the towel one more time real quick?
jokes.setup: Brett about to leave Larry's office
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate request to see Larry naked
jokes.explanation: Standard inappropriate behavior comedy. Gets a reaction but not particularly clever or character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: Can I just peek under the towel one more time real quick?

jokes.text: Oh, Bretty, you'll dream tonight.
jokes.setup: Brett wanting to peek at Larry under the towel
jokes.punchline: Artie's encouragement of her inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Artie enabling inappropriate behavior in his characteristic way. Good character voice and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, Bretty, you'll dream tonight.

jokes.text: Just buy bigger shovels and cry sometimes.
jokes.setup: Asked how she handles the horseshit that fame brings
jokes.punchline: Practical advice combining pragmatism with emotional release
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor that's both practical and emotionally honest. Memorable phrasing and good life advice disguised as comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: Just buy bigger shovels and cry sometimes.

jokes.text: Just tell yourself that everything they write about you is true. That way it won't bother you.
jokes.setup: Artie sharing Brando's advice about tabloids
jokes.punchline: Counterintuitive psychological strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reverse psychology presented as wisdom. Actually useful advice disguised as a joke. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Just tell yourself that everything they write about you is true. That way it won't bother you.

jokes.text: A man is lying on his deathbed not 5 miles from here, and these bloodsuckers, these leeches... A little respect for the soon-to-be dead, please.
jokes.setup: Hank upset about agents trying to poach him while Sid is in hospital
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic moral outrage while considering their offers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank hypocrisy - moral outrage while being tempted by the very thing he's condemning. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: A man is lying on his deathbed not 5 miles from here, and these bloodsuckers, these leeches... A little respect for the soon-to-be dead, please.

jokes.text: I guess you'd call it a lesbian thing.
jokes.setup: Brett nervously building up to revealing something about Paula
jokes.punchline: The awkward revelation of their past relationship
jokes.explanation: Good buildup and awkward delivery. The casualness of 'lesbian thing' creates the comedy through understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: I guess you'd call it a lesbian thing.

jokes.text: I've always loved women with a touch of sappho.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to be worldly about Brett's revelation
jokes.punchline: Classical reference to lesbianism trying to sound sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - trying to sound cultured and worldly. The classical reference shows his pretensions while being accepting.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I've always loved women with a touch of sappho.

jokes.text: We have k.d. lang on the show all the time. Although, the last time, Hank kept calling her 'sir.' That asshole.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to show he's comfortable with gay performers
jokes.punchline: Reveals Hank's inappropriate behavior with androgynous performer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing both Artie's tolerance and Hank's social obliviousness. Good specific celebrity reference.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: We have k.d. lang on the show all the time. Although, the last time, Hank kept calling her 'sir.' That asshole.

jokes.text: Just because you done it once doesn't make you a dyke.
jokes.setup: Brett worried about her past affecting current perception
jokes.punchline: Artie's blunt, practical wisdom using crude language
jokes.explanation: Artie's characteristic crude but supportive advice. The bluntness and practical wisdom is very much his voice.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Just because you done it once doesn't make you a dyke.

jokes.text: Sid was not from the old school. He was from the school they tore down to build the old school.
jokes.setup: Agent trying to diplomatically criticize Sid as old-fashioned
jokes.punchline: Escalates the metaphor to show Sid is ancient
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of a tired metaphor. Perfectly constructed and highly quotable insult that becomes funnier the more you think about it.

dialogue.line: Sid was not from the old school. He was from the school they tore down to build the old school.

jokes.text: He's dead. Or-- or near-dead.
jokes.setup: Hank talking to agent about Sid's condition
jokes.punchline: Hank's casual correction from dead to near-dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - exaggerating for effect then casually correcting himself. Shows his tendency toward drama.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He's dead. Or-- or near-dead.

jokes.text: Cold little bitch.
jokes.setup: Brett finishing pre-interview with Paula
jokes.punchline: Brett's harsh assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Blunt assessment that's harsh but economical. The casual cruelty fits the show's industry satire tone.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: Cold little bitch.

jokes.text: You know, she couldn't give me a good massage because half the time her tongue got caught up in her hands.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about chatty masseuse
jokes.punchline: Metaphor for talking interfering with manual dexterity
jokes.explanation: Decent metaphor for multitasking failure. Shows Larry's petty complaints about service industry workers.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You know, she couldn't give me a good massage because half the time her tongue got caught up in her hands.

jokes.text: It'll be like Regis and Kathy Lee.
jokes.setup: Brett worried about lack of pre-interview coordination
jokes.punchline: Comparison to notoriously awkward TV partnership
jokes.explanation: Good 90s reference to dysfunctional TV partnership. Solid comparison that lands the point economically.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: It'll be like Regis and Kathy Lee.

jokes.text: I found a lump on one of my testicles... I made Elke Sommer cry in the middle of a dance rehearsal.
jokes.setup: Artie relating his own medical scare experience
jokes.punchline: The specific consequence of his anxiety affecting his work
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail that shows how personal crisis affects professional behavior. Elke Sommer reference adds period authenticity.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I found a lump on one of my testicles... I made Elke Sommer cry in the middle of a dance rehearsal.

jokes.text: Because he was thinking about my left nut.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining why Bob Hope's timing was off during taping
jokes.punchline: Crude but matter-of-fact explanation of the distraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the story. The bluntness combined with the absurdity of Bob Hope being distracted by testicles is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Because he was thinking about my left nut.

jokes.text: That's the show I fucked up because of my titty.
jokes.setup: Artie's advice about not letting medical worries affect work
jokes.punchline: His crude but effective way of making the point
jokes.explanation: Artie's characteristically crude but wise advice. The word choice and delivery are perfect for his voice.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That's the show I fucked up because of my titty.

jokes.text: Just go straight up your ass and turn left.
jokes.setup: Agent asking for directions to Hank's office
jokes.punchline: Paula's hostile anatomical directions
jokes.explanation: Standard hostile response to unwanted people. Gets the job done but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Just go straight up your ass and turn left.

jokes.text: You're not all getting on the elevator at once, are you? You kids, you're so crazy!
jokes.setup: Hank dismissing the CAA agents
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank awkwardness - trying to be casual and failing. His social discomfort shows through forced joviality.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You're not all getting on the elevator at once, are you? You kids, you're so crazy!

jokes.text: It was a hiatal hernia. It's a rip in the esophagus... Fucking doctors! I mean, come on!
jokes.setup: Learning Sid is not dying, just has a hernia
jokes.punchline: Hank's anger at doctors for the misdiagnosis
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Hank's displaced anger at being put through emotional turmoil unnecessarily.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It was a hiatal hernia. It's a rip in the esophagus... Fucking doctors! I mean, come on!

jokes.text: If you say a person's not going to make it through the day, then he shouldn't make it. It's just so inconsiderate to just put us through this worry.
jokes.setup: Hank upset about medical misdiagnosis
jokes.punchline: His logic that doctors should be consistent with death predictions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that's perfectly Hank - selfish concern about his own inconvenience disguised as critique of medical practice. Quotable and character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: If you say a person's not going to make it through the day, then he shouldn't make it. It's just so inconsiderate to just put us through this worry.

jokes.text: [Hank cries]
jokes.setup: Relief that Sid is okay combined with guilt about considering new agents
jokes.punchline: Visual of Hank breaking down emotionally
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Hank's emotional overwhelm. The physical comedy of an adult man crying works well.

jokes.text: So I say, 'hoist another cup of joe to lumpy.'
jokes.setup: Paula learning to live with coffee-caused breast lumps
jokes.punchline: Naming her tumor and toasting it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical issues that shows Paula's coping mechanism. Naming the tumor 'Lumpy' is both disturbing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: So I say, 'hoist another cup of joe to lumpy.'

jokes.text: There's, like, no smoking in the office, dipshit. Fuck you. I'm, like, alive, dipshit.
jokes.setup: Workplace smoking policy enforcement
jokes.punchline: Paula's celebration of not having cancer trumps office rules
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Paula's 'fuck it' attitude after medical scare. Life perspective trumping office rules.

dialogue.line: There's, like, no smoking in the office, dipshit.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Fuck you. I'm, like, alive, dipshit.

jokes.text: Where in goddamn hell is the fire?! You scared the shit out of me! It's just the incense.
jokes.setup: Artie panicking about smoke/fire
jokes.punchline: It's just meditation incense
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke with good Artie panic reaction. Works functionally but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Where in goddamn hell is the fire?! You scared the shit out of me!
It's just the incense.

jokes.text: You are so... unbelievable. You know what I remember 10 years ago? You're very, very selfish.
jokes.setup: Brett realizing Larry's relief is about not being mad at him
jokes.punchline: Her assessment of his fundamental character flaw
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Larry's self-centeredness. Brett's disgust feels earned and specific.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: You are so... unbelievable. You know what I remember 10 years ago? You're very, very selfish.

jokes.text: I almost lost a-- I almost lost Sid today... Sid, take care of your esophagus.
jokes.setup: Hank's emotional on-air tribute to his agent
jokes.punchline: Specific medical advice delivered sentimentally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - sincere emotion combined with awkward specificity. The esophagus advice is endearingly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I almost lost a-- I almost lost Sid today... Sid, take care of your esophagus.

jokes.text: I got a massage. The woman did not stop talking... And plus, she wanted a very steep security and cleaning deposit which I did not get back afterwards.
jokes.setup: Brett describing her bad day
jokes.punchline: Reveals the massage was actually prostitution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that recontextualizes the earlier massage complaints. The 'deposit' detail clinches the revelation perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: I got a massage. The woman did not stop talking... And plus, she wanted a very steep security and cleaning deposit which I did not get back afterwards.

jokes.text: You get your mom a house and you figure whatever else you do with your money is ok if you get your mom a house.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about guilt over money
jokes.punchline: Brett's practical philosophy about wealth guilt
jokes.explanation: Good practical wisdom about celebrity wealth. Relatable and specific philosophy that feels authentic.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: You get your mom a house and you figure whatever else you do with your money is ok if you get your mom a house.

jokes.text: I'm a WASP. Wasps don't have guilt, do they? Oh, no. But they have moms.
jokes.setup: Brett explaining her lack of guilt about money
jokes.punchline: Larry's observation about WASP family dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good cultural observation with nice setup/punchline structure. Larry's response shows understanding of WASP dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: I'm a WASP. Wasps don't have guilt, do they?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, no. But they have moms.

jokes.text: Hey, see what happens when you mess around with another chick? It's bad news, huh?
jokes.setup: Brett and Paula's awkward interactions all day
jokes.punchline: Brett's casual advice about lesbian experimentation consequences
jokes.explanation: Ties together the episode's lesbian subplot with workplace awkwardness. Brett's casual delivery works well.

dialogue.speaker: Brett
dialogue.line: Hey, see what happens when you mess around with another chick? It's bad news, huh?

jokes.text: Oh, some dyke thing, I guess. Well, it appears I owe you $100.
jokes.setup: Larry dismissively explaining Brett and Paula's interaction to Artie
jokes.punchline: Reveals Artie had bet on the lesbian story being true
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reveals Artie believed the story and bet Larry money on it. Great economy and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, some dyke thing, I guess.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, it appears I owe you $100.
